ref: That's what you are like. Outside you look good, but inside you are evil and only pretend to be good.
text: Bala mɛa ti ya yiŋa vom ani tuuntɔɔ nɛreba nɛŋan, gee ti ya puan la dɛna birekɔgenɛ la ŋmɛleŋɔ.
translated: The child is crying because he wants to eat, and his mother is telling him to wait a little bit.

ref: So keep on doing everything they did.
text: Yi kɔ'ɔm tum ya ya yaabeduma tuuma la ti la pirɛ.
translated: I am going to the market to buy some food.

ref: Jesus went to the home of Peter, where he found that Peter's mother-in-law was sick in bed with fever.
text: Yezu en kɛ̃ Pita yire la, a nyɛ ti Pita dɛɛmpɔka n gã' suŋɔ zuo gbi'ira la ɔɔrɔ,
translated: Jesus said to Peter, "Truly I say to you, you are a rock, and on this rock I will build my church, and the gates of Hades will not prevail against it."

ref: When they came to Jesus, they saw the man who had once been full of demons. He was sitting there with his clothes on and in his right mind, and they were terrified.
text: Ban wa'am na paaɛ Yezu zi'an la, ba nyɛ ti buraa la ti Kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi dɔla en la, zi' mɛ, yɛ lɔgerɔ dɛna a mɛŋa. Buraa sɛka ti Kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi tuha tuha dole ni ka, ti ba zoti ni ka la en bala.
translated: When the Lord Jesus saw this, he was moved with compassion for them, because they were like sheep without a shepherd. So he began to teach them many things.

ref: The master of the servant told him, “You are lazy and good-for-nothing! You know I harvest what I don't plant and gather crops where I haven't scattered seed.
text: Ti a daana la lerege en yeti, ‘Fum dela putɔya la zinyaka. Fum mi' ti mam kɛ̃'ɛ̃ri la zi'an ti n ka burɛ, gee buna zi'an ti n ka lobege bumbuura la, vaara ze'a ti en ka lobege
translated: You should tell the young man, 'Go and work hard. If you want to be rich, don't be lazy, go and work hard. If you want to be a great man, go and work hard.'

ref: When Herod found out that the wise men from the east had tricked him, he was very angry. He gave orders for his men to kill all the boys who lived in or near Bethlehem and were two years old and younger. This was based on what he had learned from the wise men.
text: Hɛrɔd n ta baŋɛ ti baŋereba la pã'ahɛ en mɛ la, a ukɛ mɛ kankaŋi, gee bo' nuurɛ ti ba doohe kɔrum bibudibeto sɛba woo n boi Bɛtelehɛm la ka kɔrɔmkɔrɔm za'a, kɔmsɛba n de yuuma ayi' bii sɛba n pun ka paaɛ bala, doohe la hansɛka ti a wum ti baŋereba duma la tɔgɛ bo' en la.
translated: Herod, hearing this, was troubled, and all Jerusalem with him. And assembling all the chief priests and scribes of the people, he inquired of them where the Christ was to be born. They told him, “In Bethlehem of Judea, for so it is written by the prophet: ‘And you, Bethlehem, in the land of Judah, are by no means least among the rulers of Judah; for from you shall come a ruler who will shepherd my people Israel.’”

ref: You cannot be the slave of two masters! You will like one more than the other or be more loyal to one than the other. You cannot serve both God and money. Worry (Luke
text: “Nɛra kan ta'am tuna bɔ'ɔra kpɛ'ɛnduma bayi', se'ere en sɔi la, a wan ba'afum yaaɛ ayima gee nɔŋɛ ayima, bii a ba' tuna bɔ'ɔra ayima gee bahera ayima. Ya kan ta'am dɛna yameŋa bo' Yinɛ la lɔgerɔ buyina. Da fabele ya
translated: “If you want to eat fish, you must go to the river. If you want to eat meat, you must go to the bush. If you want to eat yam, you must go to the farm. If you want to eat rice, you must go to the market.”

ref: If I use the power of Beelzebul to force out demons, whose power do your own followers use to force them out? Your followers are the ones who will judge you.
text: Bala, mam han tara Beelezebule sa'aha kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi, gee yamam nɛreba la me tari la ani sa'aha kulekpa'bɛ'ɛhi? Bala zuo, ya mɛhi zahereba la en wan gakɛ ya.
translated: Bala, my father, when Beelzebub came to steal your sheep, did you not see him? Bala said, "Yes, I saw him, but I did not know it was him."

ref: Many false prophets will come and fool a lot of people.
text: Ti nɔtɔgeheba pumparenduma zo'e zo'e wan wa'am na pa'ahɛ nɛreba zo'e zo'e ti ba.
translated: I don't know how to translate this phrase. It doesn't seem to be a known language.

ref: A little while later some people standing there walked over to Peter and said, “We know you are one of them. We can tell it because you talk like someone from Galilee.”
text: Ti la iŋɛ fii wa ti nɛreba la n ze' la paaɛ Pita zi'an yele en yeti, “Yelemɛŋerɛ fum dela ba ayima. Fum tugum n pa'alɛ.”
translated: He said to them, "I tell you the truth, I am going to Jerusalem. I will suffer there at the hands of the elders, chief priests, and teachers of the law. They will put me to death, and three days later I will rise again."

ref: Jesus got up and ordered the wind and the waves to be quiet. The wind stopped, and everything was calm.
text: Ti a ihege tahɛ sabeeo la, gee yele ko'mi'ihi la yeti, “Suram! Ana bããlam!” Ti sabeeo la go'e, ti sɛla woo ma'ɛ.
translated: He went to the market and saw a woman there, and he said to her, "Hello! How are you?" The woman replied, "I am fine, thank you."

ref: Large crowds followed Jesus from Galilee and the region around the ten cities known as Decapolis. They also came from Jerusalem, Judea, and from across the Jordan River.
text: Ti nɛrekuuŋɔ dɔla a poore ze'ele Galilii, Dekapolise en de , Yerusalɛm, Yudiya, la Yɔɔdan yakenɛ ha.
translated: He traveled throughout Galilee, Decapolis, Jerusalem, Judea, and the region across the Jordan.

ref: When you pray, don't be like those show-offs who love to stand up and pray in the synagogues and on the street corners. They do this just to look good. I can assure you that they already have their reward.
text: “Ya ni han yeti ya pu'uhɛ, da ni ani ya wuu birekɔgenduma la. Bama nɔŋɛ la ba ni dooe zɛ'a saazuo zamehegɔ yiya la puan, la sadɔtɔ kãra zi'ihi woo puan, pu'uha ti nɛreba nyɛ ba. N yeti ya la yelemɛŋerɛ wa, ti ba pun to'e ba yɔɔrɛ mɛ.
translated: They have not yet finished eating, but they are already full. The children are playing with the leftovers, and the adults are resting, some are sleeping. I am tired, but I must go and fetch water.

ref: Some Pharisees wanted to test Jesus. They came up to him and asked, “Is it right for a man to divorce his wife for just any reason?”
text: Ti Farasii basɛba wa'am a zi'an na, ban boti ba makɛ en bihɛ la, ti ba soke en yeti, “La de ma'a, ti buraa lebehe a pɔga yele se'ere puan?”
translated: Why are you people always fighting? Can't you all just live in peace?

ref: Above his head they put a sign that told why he was nailed there. It read, “This is Jesus, the King of the Jews.”
text: A zuin, ba gulehɛ la gulehɛgɔ yagelɛ ti gulehɛgɔ la yeti, “Yezu en ŋwana, Yuuduma naba la.”
translated: Jesus said to them, "I am the bread of life. Whoever comes to me will never go hungry, and whoever believes in me will never be thirsty."

ref: “Listen, lands of Zebulun and Naphtali, lands along the road to the sea and across the Jordan. Listen Galilee, land of the Gentiles!
text: “Zebulun la Nafetali tihi wa en boi ateko sore zuo wa, bɔna Yɔɔdan yakenɛ, Galilii en de sɛba en dagi Yuuduma wa,
translated: Zebulun and Naphtali shall be a people burdened with toil, but they shall dwell in the west, and Jordan shall be their boundary, Galilee and the land of the sea shall be theirs.

ref: Rain poured down, rivers flooded, and the winds blew and beat against that house. Finally, it fell with a crash.
text: Ti saahi ni, ti ko'om di, ti kusebego lobe, ŋmɛ' yire la, ti yire la lu, lu damɛ wuregam!”
translated: You are strong, you are brave, you are a good person, I love you, I love you very much, very much indeed!

ref: and the one who scattered them is the devil. The harvest is the end of time, and angels are the ones who bring in the harvest.
text: dima la en burɛ mɔyileŋo la dela Sagena la, ti buŋa la me dɛna timpaberɛ wa ba'ahegɔ saŋa, ti buneba la dɛna Yinɛ suŋereba.
translated: Dima, the son of Burɛ, went to Sagena's house. He found the house empty, only the cooking pot was there. He also found Yinɛ's belongings.

ref: His disciples said, “This place is like a desert. Where can we find enough food to feed such a crowd?”
text: Ti a zahereba yele en yeti, “Bɛha ti tu wan nyɛ dia zo'e muɔ wa puan diihe nɛrekuuŋɔ kuna taaba wa?”
translated: Why are you asking me this question, "Do you know the person who stole the money from the market?"

ref: He will place the sheep on his right and the goats on his left.
text: A wan dikɛ piihi la lebe a zuɔ nu'o bɔba, gee dikɛ buuhi la me lebe a gɔbega nu'o.
translated: A man who is strong will always be respected, but a man who is weak will always be despised.
